title: pedicularis anas subsp. anas
intro: Pedicularis anas subsp. anas, also known as the Larkspur of the Alps, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the Pedicularis genus. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects due to the presence of alkaloids such as lycopsamine and lycorine. The health benefits of Pedicularis anas subsp. anas include its potential to relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in treating digestive problems and as a diuretic. Herbal preparations of Pedicularis anas subsp. anas can be found in the form of tinctures, infusions, and dried plant material, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity.
